'''Craig Mullen''' (born 15 January 1998) is a former professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who last played as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[London Skolars]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]], and the [[Swinton Lions]] and [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Mullen was born in [[Wigan, Greater Manchester]], [[England]].

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44367280|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 32-16 Wigan Warriors|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
It was announced on 18 November 2020 that Craig would join Leigh on a permanent basis for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-land-promising-utility-craig-mullen-on-permanent-deal-from-wigan/|title=Leigh land promising utility Craig Mullen on permanent deal from Wigan|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=16 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 25 Oct 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Newcastle Thunder coach Eamon O’Carroll excited by quartet of signings|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newcastle-thunder-eamon-ocarroll-excited-signings/}}</ref>
